title: Projects
text:
Boston, MA | Aug 2025 – Dec 2025 Tools: Python (Jupyter Notebook), SQL

Developed and executed complex SQL queries within Python to extract and analyze data from a relational database
Analyzed 1,500+ sales transactions across customers, employees, and product categories
Evaluated sales performance, revenue trends, and customer purchasing behavior
Built dashboards and visualizations to highlight revenue drivers and customer segments
Translated analytical findings into actionable insights to support sales strategy and decision-making





Boston, MA | Mar 2025 – May 2025 Tools: Python, AWS EC2, SQL, GitHub, VS Code

Analyzed 71,000+ job records to evaluate salary and compensation trends across industries, regions, and work arrangements
Deployed a cloud-based analytics environment using AWS EC2 to enable scalable data processing
Conducted exploratory data analysis (EDA) to identify patterns in compensation, remote work trends, and geographic differences
Assessed the impact of experience, role type, and technical skills on salary outcomes
Developed analytical models for salary benchmarking and trend forecasting, delivering actionable workforce insights





Boston, MA | Oct 2024 – Dec 2024 Tools: Power BI, Random Forest (R), Forecasting (R), Ggplot 2 (R), Predicting Modeling (R)

Analyzed 2,500+ residential property records to assess housing prices and demand trends
Built and evaluated Random Forest models to estimate property values and identify key drivers
Performed forecasting to assess short-term market trends and expected sale outcomes
Cleaned and validated datasets to improve data quality and model performance
Delivered insights to support pricing strategy and market segmentation





Boston, MA | Jan 2025 – Mar 2025 Tools: Data Analysis, Cost-Benefit Analysis, Performance Metrics

Analyzed operational data from 50 bus routes to evaluate service reliability and delays
Identified opportunities for 10% cost reduction and 15% efficiency improvement
Developed performance metrics and monitoring frameworks for transit operations
Integrated multiple data sources to support structured reporting
Provided insights to support data-driven operational and planning decisions





Boston, MA | Aug 2024 – Nov 2024 Tools: Financial Modeling, Break-Even Analysis, Sensitivity Analysis, Market Research

Conducted a 3-year financial feasibility analysis for a $150K investment in a microbrewery
Modeled production capacity of 476K+ pints annually and evaluated operational feasibility
Built demand forecasts covering 400K+ projected units over three years
Performed market analysis and customer segmentation to support positioning strategy
Delivered investment recommendations based on profitability and long-term growth potential

title: An Ly
text:
👋 Hi, I’m An Ly
“The world moves fast, but I lead with positivity.”
I’m a graduate student in Applied Business Analytics at Boston University, and I hold a Bachelor’s degree in Global Business and Entrepreneurship from Suffolk University.
I am passionate about leveraging data to support strategic decision-making and enhance organizational performance. My areas of interest include Product analytics, Data visualization & dashboarding (e.g., Tableau, Power BI), Supply chain & operations analytics, Financial analytics & forecasting, Marketing analytics & customer insights, etc.
As an early-career professional, I am actively seeking opportunities where I can apply analytical thinking and technical skills to drive data-informed decisions and deliver measurable business impact.


 📖 See More About Me




💼 Projects
Welcome to my project showcase! These projects demonstrate my skills in analytics, strategy, visualization, and business modeling.
📁 View Projects



📄 CV
Here’s a summary of my education, experience, and technical skills.
📄 View CV



📬 Contact
Let’s connect! I’m always open to new opportunities, collaborations, and conversations.

title: About Me
text:
An Ly – Profile Photo


I am an aspiring business analytics professional with hands-on experience in data analysis, statistical modeling, and business intelligence. I have worked with tools such as Python, SQL, R, and data visualization platforms to analyze large datasets, identify trends, and translate complex information into actionable business insights.
I am particularly strong in analytical thinking, problem-solving, and communicating data-driven insights in a clear and business-focused manner. I thrive in environments that require critical thinking and continuous learning, and I am committed to further developing my technical and analytical capabilities through real-world applications.
I am open to opportunities across diverse industries and business functions, where I can contribute to data-driven decision-making and create meaningful impact.




📊 Applying data analytics to solve business problems and optimize decision-making
🌐 Exploring the role of data in improving efficiency, performance, and strategic outcomes across industries
🧠 Actively expanding my expertise through practical experience, continuous learning, and engagement with the analytics community




Outside of working with data, you’ll probably find me:

🧳 Exploring new cultures and environments through travel and outdoor activities

⚽ Keeping up with soccer and enjoying live matches or highlights
🏀 Playing sports like soccer and basketball

🎙️ Tuning into podcasts about business, investing, or soccer news

🌱 Getting involved in student organizations, networking events, or community activities







Here are some snapshots of the amazing people and memories that keep me grounded, inspired, and motivated throughout my journey.


   






Whether I’m skiing in the winter, exploring art, or traveling to new places, I believe experiences outside the classroom shape who I am just as much as my academic journey.


   






Feel free to explore the rest of my portfolio or reach out via:

📧 honganly@bu.edu

🔗 LinkedIn – an-ly

💻 GitHub – hongan2908

I’d love to connect!
